articles.title: Despite all-Argentinian referee row, France extend astonishing World Cup record vs Morocco
articles.description: France defeated Morocco 2-0 in a World Cup quarter-final match. This victory extended France's record of winning matches officiated by Argentinian referees. The appointment of an all-Argentinian refereeing team had sparked controversy before the game. Despite the debate, France secured their win and advanced to the next stage. FIFA defended the integrity and independence of its match officials.

articles.title: Deleted chats & calls to girlfriend: How US cops caught Telangana techie for wife's murder
articles.description: A husband faces murder charges in Washington after his wife's death was initially ruled sudden. Autopsy revealed Raajitha Sabbineni died from strangulation, leading to Avinash Narne's arrest. Investigators allege Narne had an affair and contacted another woman on the day of the incident. He reportedly sent her a photograph of his deceased wife's body. The investigation continues as community members express shock at the allegations.

articles.title: Heat of Europe's AC ban comes to New York; Mayor Mamdani shares AC tips
articles.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani urged residents to reduce electricity use.  He asked people to adjust AC settings and limit unnecessary power consumption.  The city is also implementing similar measures in government buildings.  Officials aim to reduce pressure on the power grid during the heat wave.  This appeal comes as electricity demand rises across New York City.

Florida airport officially renamed after Donald Trump; US Prez calls it ‘a very big day’
articles.description: Palm Beach International Airport officially became President Donald J. Trump International Airport on Thursday. The renaming legislation was signed earlier this year by Florida Governor Ron DeSantis. Trump Force One, owned by The Trump Organisation, was the first aircraft to land. Eric Trump expressed pride in the honor bestowed upon his father. The renaming is part of a broader effort to place Trump's name on public infrastructure.

articles.title: Gold, silver rate today: Yellow metal steadies; silver tumbles
articles.full_text: Gold is headed for its second straight weekly decline as renewed tensions between the US and Iran continue to fuel inflation concerns and strengthen expectations of higher US interest rates. The prospect of tighter monetary policy has limited gains in the precious metal despite safe-haven demand. Silver prices edged higher on Friday, with spot silver rising 0.8% to $60.46 per ounce. Despite the recovery, the metal remained on track to post a weekly decline as investors continued to assess the impact of rising interest rate expectations. Gold prices inched higher on Friday as the US dollar softened, making bullion more affordable for overseas buyers. Spot gold rose 0.2% to $4,128.92 per ounce, while US gold futures for August delivery held steady at $4,139.50 per ounce. Despite the uptick, bullion remained on track for a weekly decline of more than 1%. Gold prices in the national capital fell for the fourth consecutive session on Thursday, slipping Rs 350 to Rs 1,48,100 per 10 grams. Traders attributed the decline to a stronger rupee and subdued local demand, even as international bullion prices moved higher. The appreciation of the Indian currency reduced the cost of imported gold, keeping domestic prices under pressure. Minutes from the Federal Reserve's June policy meeting showed growing concern among policymakers about persistent inflation, with a few officials seeing a case for raising interest rates. Investors are closely tracking comments from Fed officials and upcoming US economic data for fresh clues on the central bank's policy trajectory. Fresh military exchanges between the United States and Iran have reinforced concerns over inflation, prompting markets to raise expectations of a Federal Reserve rate hike. According to the CME FedWatch Tool, traders are now pricing in a 64% probability of a September rate increase, up from around 54% a week ago. Higher interest rates generally reduce the appeal of non-yielding assets such as gold. Silver prices continued to decline in the domestic market, dropping Rs 7,800 to Rs 2,32,000 per kilogram on Thursday. Although international silver prices recovered after the previous session's sharp sell-off, weak local demand and the stronger rupee weighed on domestic sentiment. Investors remain focused on global market trends and upcoming US economic data for further direction. Gold prices steadied in early trade on Friday as investors assessed the impact of renewed US-Iran tensions on inflation and interest rate expectations. Spot gold held near $4,122 per ounce, but remained on course for a weekly decline of more than 1%. Markets continue to balance safe-haven demand against expectations that the US Federal Reserve could keep interest rates higher for longer.

articles.title: Crude dips but eyes weekly gain as US-Iran conflict stokes supply fears
articles.description: Oil prices saw a slight decrease but are set for significant weekly gains. Renewed military exchanges between the United States and Iran kept crude supply concerns elevated. The conflict has delayed the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical energy chokepoint. Worries over slowing global demand and inflation are limiting sharper price rallies. Producer price inflation in China surged, increasing pressure on manufacturers.

articles.title: Meet the IIT Kanpur graduate who became an IPS officer, won a Gallantry Medal, and is now leaving the service at 37
articles.description: Jagmohan Meena, a 2013-batch Odisha cadre IPS officer and IIT Kanpur graduate, has decided to resign from the service at 37, citing personal reasons and long-term career planning. Born in Rajasthan's Sikar district, he secured AIR 849 in the 2012 UPSC Civil Services Examination and later received the Police Medal for Gallantry in 2019 for anti-Maoist operations. Meena plans to explore private-sector opportunities after completing the formal resignation process.

articles.title: TS EAMCET 2026 Phase 1 seat allotment to be released today; online self-reporting begins after allotment
articles.description: The Telangana Council of Higher Education is scheduled to release the TS EAMCET (TG EAPCET) 2026 Phase 1 seat allotment result today, July 10, 2026. Candidates can check their provisional allotment status on the official counselling portal after the link is activated. Those allotted seats must complete online self-reporting and tuition fee payment between July 10 and July 14. Phase 2 counselling registration will begin on July 17, while physical reporting is not required during Phase 1.

articles.title: Are schools closed in Telangana today? Private schools suspend classes amid bandh
articles.description: Many private and corporate schools across Telangana are closed today, July 10, 2026, because of a statewide bandh organised by student groups, while the state government has not declared an official holiday. Government schools have received no closure notification, although protests may affect normal operations. The bandh has been called over school consolidation plans, fee regulation, and vacancies in the education department. Parents have been advised to verify school operations directly with individual institutions.

articles.title: Karnataka UUCMS semester results 2026 declared at uucms.karnataka.gov.in; UG and PG students can download digital marks cards here
articles.description: The Department of Higher Education, Government of Karnataka, has announced the UUCMS 2026 semester results for undergraduate and postgraduate courses across participating state universities. Students can download their provisional digital marks cards by logging into the unified portal with their enrolment number and password. The platform displays internal assessment and theory examination marks together. Authorities have also provided helpline and email support for login issues, data discrepancies and revaluation-related technical problems.

articles.title: Dhamaal 4 Movie Review and Release Live Updates
articles.full_text: The beloved characters of Adi (Played by Arshad Warsi) and Manav (Played by Jaaved Jaaferi) from the popular 'Dhamaal' franchise enjoy a separate fanbase. Actress Sanjeeda Shaikh, who has joined the 'Dhamaal' family for the latest franchise, 'Dhamaal 4', also seems to be a fan of their natural chemistry and comic timing. Spilling her excitement about becoming a part of the beloved franchise, the 'Heeramandi' actress shared, “Adi Manav Jodi is the most dynamic duo, and nobody can beat their chemistry. My character entering their universe becomes all the more chaotic and fun. It’s funny how differently they think in different situations. They are partners in chaos, and that’s where the madness begins.” The makers treated the movie buffs to a captivating trailer of the sequel on June 12. The gang reunites to go on one of the biggest treasure hunts of their lives, taking on a journey full of hilarious misadventures, unexpected turns, and some massive hurdles. The makers of ‘Dhamaal 4’ have released their latest song, ‘Saree’, after they released their other two songs 'Qeher' and 'Chatni', a few days ago. Packed with fun, chemistry and charm, the song gives audiences a peek into the love story of Riteish Deshmukh and Anjali Anand. The song is sung by Sanju Rathod, with upbeat lyrics penned by Kumaar and Sanju Rathod. The music is composed by Tanishk Bagchi and Sanju Rathod. Speaking about the song, singer Sanju Rathod shared, “When the makers approached me to sing Saree for Dhamaal 4, I was genuinely thrilled. Dhamaal is such a loved franchise, so being associated with it is a very special feeling for me. The love that audiences have showered on my music has been overwhelming, and I hope Saree creates the same magic and and becomes a fan favourite.” The song was unveiled at an event, where 800 women in pink (gulabi) sarees joined the celebration with Riteish Deshmukh and Anjali Anand, grooving to the track in presence of fans and media alike. 'Dhamaal 4', one of the year's most anticipated comedy releases, arrives in theatres on July 10, backed by strong pre-release buzz, a star-studded ensemble and the enduring popularity of the franchise. Even before its release, reports suggest the makers may already be looking ahead to the next chapter. According to a report by Bollywood Hungama, 'Dhamaal 4' concludes with a scene that hints at the possibility of 'Dhamaal 5'. While the makers have not made any official announcement, the reported ending is said to tease another instalment in the long-running comedy series. If the sequel moves forward, it is expected to retain the franchise's trademark blend of slapstick humour, over-the-top chaos and adventure that has made the previous films audience favourites. The 'Dhamaal' franchise began in 2007 and went on to expand with 'Double Dhamaal' (2011), 'Total Dhamaal' (2019), and now 'Dhamaal 4', cementing its place as one of Bollywood's most successful comedy franchises. Actress Anjali Anand has opened up about her experience working with Riteish Deshmukh on the sets of ‘Dhamaal 4.’ In an exclusive interview with IANS, she shared a light-hearted moment from her first interaction with Riteish. The actress revealed how she broke the ice with him, making the shoot environment more comfortable and enjoyable. When asked if there was any memorable moment while working with the Masti actor, Anjali stated, “With Ritesh, I think the most memorable moment was when we met. And I just told him how crazy I am about all his films and dialogues. And he was shocked that I know all his dialogues.” “And the ice break moment was when I told him, Hey baby, there's a scene where he's dancing. That's a honeymoon line. That was my favorite thing. So that was a very funny thing.” The trailer of the fourth instalment from the 'Dhamaal' franchise is finally out, starring an ensemble cast of Ajay Devgn, Arshad Warsi and others in the lead roles. The trailer marks the return of the Dhamaal gang as they embark on the biggest treasure hunt of their lives. Tapping into nostalgia, Dhamaal 4 is packed with iconic comic moments and memorable dialogues that pay tribute to the franchise's first instalment. The three-minute-and-twenty-seven-second trailer opens with the announcement of a hidden treasure, setting Ajay Devgn, Arshad Warsi, Riteish Deshmukh, and Ravi Kishan on an adventurous quest to find it. Filled with slapstick humour, the trailer features several laugh-out-loud moments, including Jaaved Jaaferi's hilarious encounter with an alligator and Ajay Devgn's amusing reference to his iconic film Phool Aur Kaante, among many others. Promising a rib-tickling entertainer for audiences of all ages, 'Dhamaal 4' brings together an ensemble cast featuring Ajay Devgn, Arshad Warsi, Riteish Deshmukh, Jaaved Jaaferi, and Sanjay Mishra, along with Esha Gupta, Sanjeeda Shaikh, Anjali Anand, Upendra Limaye, Vijay Patkar and Ravi Kishan. Director Indra Kumar, who is gearing up for the release of his upcoming film ‘Dhamaal 4’, has shared that the toughest part of making a film is putting it on paper. The director spoke with IANS along with Jaaved Jaaferi and Arshad Warsi during the promotions of the film in the city. He told IANS, “It takes too much effort when it comes to writing and setting it up. But once the actors enter the frame, it becomes effortless. They perform it so well that the entirety of production is a smoothsail but to put the story down on paper is the toughest task in the journey of film making, I feel”. Actor Jaaved Jaaferi, who is gearing up for the release of his upcoming film ‘Dhamaal 4’, has revealed if he was ever hit with monotony while portraying his character of Manav in the film. The actor spoke with IANS during the promotions of the film along with director Indra Kumar and actor Arshad Warsi. While portraying a character for over a period of time, an actor gets a better grip on the character, and knows what clicks and what doesn’t with the audience. When asked if being very sure about the character takes the fun away, he said, “It's more fun because you are enjoying the character. You are trying to think, while remaining within the parameters of that character You constantly think, ‘What more can I add?’ without going out of the parameters. The main thing is, you have to stay within the boundaries of the character”. He told IANS, “All the actors understand the limits if the characters that they portray. Rest, the writing is so good that it brings out a good performance naturally. When the writing is not good, the actor has two problems”. He then recollected, “During my father's time, I have seen people saying, ‘Sir, you do something I have got the hero for only 2 hours, you do something’. But what can we do if the source material isn’t given to us?”. “Making a new Gen Z joke is not ‘Dhamaal’. If a Gen Z kid comes then he might add that character. Our character can't become a Gen Z”, he added. Actor Arshad Warsi, who is gearing up for the release of his upcoming film ‘Dhamaal 4’, has said that his humour comes from the gut, and he doesn’t think a lot about landing the punches. The actor spoke with IANS during the promotions of the film in the city, and shared that putting too much thought in a joke kills its impact. He told IANS, “Honestly, it's a gut feeling. We do a lot of jokes, which we think people will like it but, they don't. Some jokes are not that funny but people will like it. You really don't know what will work or what won’t. All you can do is try and do your best, and hope that that works”. “Fortunately, it has worked. The first time we did ‘Dhamaal’ that was a risk. At that time, characters were being made, our director was thinking how to do it, how to go about it or how my character will be, and what will be their nature and behavior. Once it was set, we now just follow that. And, we all do it very well”, he added.

articles.title: 'Godzilla Minus Zero' and 'Ramayana' to clash on Diwali - WATCH
articles.description: The teaser trailer for Godzilla Minus Zero has been released by makers. This highly anticipated sequel will debut in November, continuing the monster saga. Director Takashi Yamazaki confirmed this film marks the end of his Godzilla Minus saga. The movie picks up in 1949, two years after the first film's events. Godzilla Minus Zero is scheduled for release in Japan and the United States.

articles.description: Atharvaa and Kavin met Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay recently. Both actors shared their happiness and appreciation on social media platforms. The meeting generated buzz as both actors prepare for their upcoming projects.

articles.description: Three accomplished actors have joined the expanding ensemble cast for 'Alien: Earth' Season 2. Tracey Ullman, Sam Spruell, and Jerome Flynn bring considerable experience to the production. They will appear alongside previously announced new cast member Peter Dinklage. Production on the second season is now underway in London. The expanded cast has begun their work on the series.
